Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (sports betting)
Identification.ShortTitle: Safe and Regulated Sports Betting Act

Provision.Text: This enactment amends paragraph 207(4)(b) of the Criminal Code to make it lawful for the government of a province, or a person or entity licensed by the Lieutenant Governor in Council of that province, to conduct and manage a lottery scheme in the province that involves betting on a race — other than a horse-race — or fight, or on a single sport event or athletic contest.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Safe and Regulated Sports Betting Act.
Paragraph 207(4)(b) of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: bookmaking, pool selling or the making or recording of bets, including bets made through the agency of a pool or pari-mutuel system, on any horse-race; or

Section.Text: This Act comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
